strands.title: Task Structure and Scope
strands.content: A comprehensive code review of the pichay codebase is requested across exactly 7 source files (proxy.py, pager.py, eval.py, replay.py, analyzer.py, trimmer.py, __main__.py) in src/pichay/ and test files in tests/. The review must assess three dimensions for source files (code quality, potential bugs/edge cases, cross-module consistency) and two dimensions for test files (coverage gaps, false-pass risks). Output must be specific with line numbers and file paths, formatted to review.md in project root. This is a READ-and-ASSESS task, not refactor. Execution phase progressed: cycle 2 signaled file I/O readiness; cycle 3 shows [tool_use: Read] but no actual file contents have been transmitted or integrated. The system architecture remains ambiguous—whether files will arrive in a subsequent message, whether file access is stalled, or whether the [tool_use: Read] annotation signals an incomplete operation.

key_claims.text: The review scope includes exactly 7 source files and test files in tests/
The review must produce findings with specific line numbers and file paths
Five distinct assessment dimensions are required (code quality, bugs/edge cases, consistency, coverage, false passes)
File retrieval/reading operation has been invoked but contents have not been delivered or integrated

strands.title: Assessment Dimensions and Criteria
strands.content: The review evaluates source code across five dimensions: (1) Code quality—unused imports, dead code, type errors, style inconsistencies; (2) Potential bugs and unhandled edge cases—logical errors, boundary conditions, missing error handling; (3) Cross-module consistency—naming conventions, error handling patterns, API contracts between modules. For tests: (4) Missing coverage for important code paths—untested branches, unexercised error handlers; (5) False-positive risks—tests asserting incorrect behavior, mocking that masks bugs, insufficient assertions. These dimensions remain operationalized and await application to actual code content once files arrive.

key_claims.text: Code quality assessment includes unused imports, dead code, type errors, and style issues
Bug/edge-case assessment focuses on logical errors, boundary conditions, and error handling completeness
Cross-module consistency assessment spans naming conventions, error patterns, and API contracts
Test review must identify both coverage gaps and false-positive risks from weak assertions or mocking

strands.title: Execution Progress and File I/O State
strands.content: Cycle 3 introduces a critical state change: the explicit signal [tool_use: Read] indicates a read operation has been invoked. However, this is a METADATA MARKER, not a content delivery. No actual file contents, directory listings, error messages, or I/O status reports have arrived. This represents a fundamental execution bottleneck. Three interpretations remain viable: (a) files are en route and will arrive in next message; (b) file I/O system is awaiting backend completion and no status has been reported; (c) the assistant lacks file access capability and [tool_use: Read] is a placeholder indicating constraint. The pipeline cannot advance to analysis phase until actual code content is delivered. The tensor is now in STALLED state relative to its task—structure is sound, criteria are defined, but the execution path is blocked at the I/O boundary.

key_claims.text: A read operation has been explicitly invoked ([tool_use: Read])
No actual file contents, errors, or I/O status have been delivered as of cycle 3
The execution pipeline is blocked at the file I/O boundary and cannot proceed to analysis
The system may lack direct file access capability or may be experiencing backend delays

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Any actual code content, file listings, or directory structure from src/pichay/ and tests/
declared_losses.why: The [tool_use: Read] marker signals an operation attempt but no actual file data has been transmitted. Without code content, analysis cannot begin.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Confirmation of file existence, accessibility, or I/O error status
declared_losses.why: No explicit status report (success/failure) has been provided. It is unknown whether read operations succeeded, failed, or are still pending.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Ability to progress to code analysis and review generation phases
declared_losses.why: The critical dependency (file contents) has not been satisfied. All downstream work remains blocked until files arrive.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Cycle efficiency and forward progress on the actual task
declared_losses.why: Three cycles have elapsed (meta-parsing → read-invocation → read-stall). No code has been analyzed. The tensor structure is complete but cannot be applied.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Specificity about what 'Read' means in this execution context
declared_losses.why: The annotation is ambiguous—could mean 'attempted read,' 'completed read,' 'pending read,' or 'read capability marker.' No clarification provided.

open_questions: Have all 7 source files (proxy.py, pager.py, eval.py, replay.py, analyzer.py, trimmer.py, __main__.py) been successfully read from src/pichay/?
Have all test files in tests/ been identified and read?
What does the [tool_use: Read] annotation mean—is a read operation in progress, completed, or pending?
If file reads have failed, what are the specific error messages or access restrictions?
Will file contents be delivered in the next cycle, or should the review task be restructured to work without direct file access?
Does the execution environment support file I/O at all, or must the user provide file contents manually?
What is the actual Python version, framework dependencies, and architecture of pichay?
Are there established project standards (linting rules, type hints, docstring requirements) that should shape the review?
What constitutes 'completeness' for the review—does it require 100% code coverage analysis or sampling of key modules?
instructions_for_next: CRITICAL PATH DECISION POINT: Cycle 3 stalled at I/O boundary. Next cycle must do ONE of the following: (A) DELIVER FILE CONTENTS—If backend read completed, provide actual code for all 7 source files and test directory. Integrate line-by-line into analysis. BEGIN ASSESSMENT PHASE. (B) REPORT I/O FAILURE—If files cannot be accessed, explicitly state reason (permissions, path error, file not found, access disabled). Recommend alternative (user-provided content, offline analysis). (C) REQUEST USER INPUT—If execution environment does not support autonomous file access, ask user to paste file contents or provide directory tree. (D) CLARIFY CAPABILITY—If [tool_use: Read] is incomplete/unknown state, explicitly confirm whether system can/cannot read files. DO NOT proceed to cycle 4 without resolving the I/O bottleneck. The tensor structure is sound but application is impossible without code content. Highest priority: move from STALLED to ANALYZING state. If files cannot be obtained, pivot the task rather than carrying forward a blocked pipeline.
